content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the cumulative code changes excluding `.ralph-burning/` and found 1 fix-now correctness gap in the non-Linux legacy PID reuse logic.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `src/adapters/fs.rs:235-238` drops `ProcessStartPrecision` on the floor, and `legacy_pid_record_live_state()` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:820-845`) treats any live process whose derived start time is `<= record.started_at` as definitively `Live`. On macOS/BSD, `process_started_at()` only has whole-second precision (`src/adapters/fs.rs:323-328`), so a PID reused later in the same second as the original `run.pid` write is still accepted as the original process. That means legacy pid files can still be misclassified as live after PID reuse on the non-Linux platforms this bead is supposed to harden, causing resume/recovery/cleanup to wait on an unrelated process.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/adapters/fs.rs"]
remediation_hint: Make the coarse-grained path conservative. When the live start time is only known to whole-second precision, same-second comparisons should return `RunningUnverified` (or equivalent) rather than `Live`; only timestamps at least one full second after the rounded start should be treated as a definite match.
   Rationale: The acceptance criteria explicitly require safe PID reuse handling on macOS/BSD. As written, the non-Linux legacy fallback still has a same-second false-positive window because it rounds the live start time down to seconds and then ignores that loss of precision.
